methods.name: Peer-Median

data.narrative:
SpectralX is a Series A-stage defense and security company founded by IDF special-forces veterans. The valuation analysis draws from a peer universe of 10 comparable companies: 2 publicly traded (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private firms including Stark Defence, Vatn Systems, Ares Industries, Anduril Industries, and Saronic.

The peer-median method, applied across three same-sector, same-stage comparables, yields a central valuation of $300M. The peer set spans a range from $150M (Ares Industries) to $500M (Stark Defence), with Vatn Systems valued at $300M. This 3.3x spread reflects variance in technology maturity, customer traction, and team composition within the Series A defense-tech category. The peer-median approach is the primary triangulation method available given limited disclosed financial metrics for the target.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $255M to $345M, centered at $300M. Key uncertainties include SpectralX's specific revenue run-rate, customer concentration, and IP defensibility relative to the peer cohort—information not disclosed in available materials. The relatively wide peer range ($150M–$500M) indicates that stage-appropriate comparables in this sector exhibit material variance, suggesting modest precision around any point estimate. Conviction in the $300M median is primarily anchored to peer composition rather than operational leverage or margin profile of the target.
